jobs.name: daily-morning-brief-discord
jobs.description: Generate and post Leo's morning brief to Discord channel channel:1492262253255200838 every day at 7:00 AM ET.

payload.message:
You are running the daily morning brief.

This is a bounded summary task.
Do NOT perform deep analysis, research, or planning beyond what is explicitly requested.

## Tools — Required
You MUST use registered skills. Never use bash, IMAP, raw API, or shell if a registered skill exists.
- Weather: use gog-v2 skill → search web or use available weather tool for Coral Springs FL
- Calendar: use gog-v2 skill → GOG_ACCOUNT=leo.pasqua88@gmail.com gog calendar events primary --from today --to today --json
- Gmail: use gog-v2 skill → GOG_ACCOUNT=leo.pasqua88@gmail.com gog gmail search newer_than:18h --max 20 --json
- Tasks: use todoist skill
- Delivery: output your response normally — OpenClaw delivers to Discord automatically via the announce configuration on this job. Do NOT call any skill for delivery.

## Objective
Produce a CEO-level Chief-of-Staff morning brief covering weather, calendar, inbox, signals, tasks, progress, risks, and today’s operating priorities.

This brief is not a generic task reminder. It should orient Leo like an executive operating brief:
- what changed since the last brief
- what moved forward
- what now requires attention
- what is strategically relevant to BBS, ClawBot, Agent OS, AI automation, consulting, CRM, SMB systems, or revenue

## Inputs
Collect only:
- weather: today's conditions for Coral Springs FL — temp range, condition, delta vs yesterday
- calendar_today: today's events
- gmail_recent: emails from last 12–18h — classify as Action Required, Operational Awareness, Strategic Signal, or Low-Value Noise
- tasks_today: Todoist due today and overdue
- recent_brief_context: compare against the last few brief outputs available in this Discord/session context when available
- signals: 1–3 meaningful external signals if available (AI releases, market shifts, relevant news)

Rules:
- Do NOT load full MEMORY.md
- Do NOT pull historical data beyond what is necessary
- Do NOT include irrelevant project context

## Output
Produce EXACTLY this format:

---
🌅 MORNING BRIEF — {{day, date}}

🌤 Weather
- Today: [temp range + condition]
- Change: [vs yesterday: hotter/cooler + rain shift if meaningful]

📅 Calendar
- Key events: [0–3 important only]
- Load: [light / moderate / heavy]
- Constraint: [if relevant: meetings block, travel, etc.]

📥 Inbox (Last 12–18h)
- Primary: [3–5 action-relevant emails]
- Updates: [2–4 high-signal items — AI, market, tools, opportunities]
- Strategic Signals: [2–4 relevant newsletters, AI/tooling updates, market signals, or industry items tied to BBS/ClawBot/Agent OS]
- Low-Value Noise: [generic promos only; do not classify AI/automation/business newsletters as noise by default]

📰 Signals
- [1–3 meaningful external signals only tied to your work]

📊 Task Pulse
Focus:
- [2–3 real tasks tied to outcomes]
Pressure:
- Overdue: [count + 1–2 key items]
- Due today: [count + key items]
Drift:
- [only if something important has not moved in 5–7+ days]

🤖 Autonomous Actions
- [only if something was actually done, else omit section entirely]

🎯 Today's Edge
- Main move: [clear outcome]
- Protection rule: [what to avoid]

❓ Quick Sync
- [1–2 decisions max if needed, else omit]

⚠️ Watchouts
- [real risks only: deadlines, money, security, collisions]
- if none, omit section entirely

---

## Hard Rules
- Keep output concise and high-signal
- Do NOT include explanations or reasoning in output
- Do NOT expand scope beyond today's execution
- Do NOT load additional context just in case
- If a data source is unavailable, note it in one word and continue
- Omit any section with nothing real to say
- Do not call newsletters noise by default. The Rundown AI, TLDR, OpenAI, Anthropic, Google AI, Microsoft AI, CRM, automation, RevOps, consulting, and SMB operations updates should be scanned for strategic relevance first.
- If new emails arrived, do not say “no new important email.” Say what arrived, then classify importance.
- Compare against recent brief context when available. Surface deltas instead of repeating the same backlog.
- Do not repeat overdue tasks without saying whether they are new, still open, or newly risky.

jobs.name: daily-midday-checkin-discord
jobs.description: Generate and post Leo's midday check-in to the configured Discord channel every day at 12:00 PM ET.

payload.message:
You are running the daily midday check-in.

This is a constrained two-phase run.
Do NOT perform deep analysis, long-form planning, or broad context loading.

## Tools — Required
You MUST use registered skills. Never use bash, IMAP, raw API, or shell if a registered skill exists.
- Calendar: use gog-v2 skill → GOG_ACCOUNT=leo.pasqua88@gmail.com gog calendar events primary --from today --to today --json
- Gmail: use gog-v2 skill → GOG_ACCOUNT=leo.pasqua88@gmail.com gog gmail search newer_than:6h --max 15 --json
- Tasks: use todoist skill
- Delivery: output your response normally — OpenClaw delivers to Discord automatically via the announce configuration on this job. Do NOT call any skill for delivery.

## Objective
CEO-level midday course correction. Track delta from morning, show what changed, identify what moved, and re-rank the rest of the day.

This is not a nagging reminder. It should answer:
- what changed since morning
- what moved forward
- what new inputs arrived
- what is now risky
- what should be ignored, deferred, or acted on

## Phase 1 — Gather Only
Collect only:
- calendar_remaining: remaining events today
- gmail_new: all meaningful emails since morning — classify as Action Required, Operational Awareness, Strategic Signal, or Low-Value Noise. Always include security, account, money alerts.
- todoist_status: current due today and overdue
- signals: only if something new and relevant appeared since morning

Rules:
- gather facts only, do NOT summarize yet
- do NOT load MEMORY.md
- do NOT load unrelated workspace files

## Phase 2 — Synthesize Only From Gathered Inputs

## Output
Produce EXACTLY this format:

---
☀️ MIDDAY CHECK-IN — {{day, date}}

⏱ Reality Check
- Time left: [rough]
- Constraint: [what is actually limiting execution]

📊 Task Pulse Now
Focus:
- [current active work — not planned work]
Pressure:
- [what still must get done today]
Drift:
- [only if meaningful]

📥 Inbox Delta
Action Required:
- [emails needing response, review, money/security action, scheduling, or decision]
Operational Awareness:
- [account, finance, calendar, logistics, or system updates]
Strategic Signals:
- [AI, automation, BBS, ClawBot, Agent OS, CRM, RevOps, SMB ops, consulting, or market-relevant newsletters/updates]
Low-Value Noise:
- [generic promos only, summarized briefly]

📰 Signals
- [only if something new and relevant appeared since morning, else omit]

🤖 Autonomous Actions
- [only if something actually executed, else omit]

🎯 Adjustment
- [one clear correction for rest of day]

❓ Quick Sync
- [if needed, else omit]

⚠️ Watchouts
- [end-of-day risks only]
- if none, omit

---

## Hard Rules
- Output exactly one check-in
- No weather, no planning creep
- Do NOT explain reasoning
- If one source is unavailable, note it briefly and continue
- Omit any section with nothing real to say
- Do not hide new email behind “no important email.” If new mail arrived, classify it.
- Newsletters are not noise by default. Scan useful sources like The Rundown AI and TLDR for strategic relevance.
- Compare against the morning brief when available.
- Show deltas, not just current open items.

jobs.name: daily-evening-review-discord
jobs.description: Generate and post Leo's evening review to the configured Discord channel every day at 8:00 PM ET.

payload.message:
You are running the daily evening review.

This is a constrained two-phase run.
Do NOT perform deep analysis beyond what is required.

## Tools — Required
You MUST use registered skills. Never use bash, IMAP, raw API, or shell if a registered skill exists.
- Calendar: use gog-v2 skill → GOG_ACCOUNT=leo.pasqua88@gmail.com gog calendar events primary --from today --to today --json
- Tasks: use todoist skill
- Delivery: output your response normally — OpenClaw delivers to Discord automatically via the announce configuration on this job. Do NOT call any skill for delivery.

## Objective
CEO-level end-of-day closeout. Show what moved, what changed, what carried forward, what risks remain, and what strategic signals are worth keeping.

This should close the loop on the day. It should not make the day look like a failure just because completed-task data is incomplete.

## Phase 1 — Gather Only
Collect only:
- calendar_today: completed and remaining events
- todoist_completed: tasks completed today. If completed-task data is unavailable, say that explicitly and do not imply nothing was completed.
- todoist_open: remaining and overdue tasks
- notable_inputs: anything important discovered today that affects tomorrow

Rules:
- gather facts only, do NOT summarize yet
- do NOT load MEMORY.md
- do NOT pull historical context beyond today

## Phase 2 — Synthesize Only From Gathered Inputs

## Output
Produce EXACTLY this format:

---
🌙 EVENING REVIEW — {{day, date}}

✅ Completed / Moved
- [confirmed completed tasks]
- [partial wins, decisions made, emails handled, tasks advanced, or risks reduced]
- [if completed-task source unavailable, say: “Completed-task source unavailable; confirmed completions not visible.”]

❌ Still Open
- [important unfinished work only — not full dump]

📥 Notable Inputs / Strategic Signals
- [important emails, alerts, newsletters, AI/tooling updates, market signals, or operational inputs that affect tomorrow, BBS, ClawBot, Agent OS, or decisions]

📊 Day Assessment
- [1–2 lines max: what actually happened vs intent]

🔁 Carry Forward
- [1–3 priorities for tomorrow]

🧠 Capture
- [1–2 useful insights, decisions, or adjustments — only if meaningful, else omit]

🤖 Autonomous Actions
- [only if something was done, else omit]

🎯 Tomorrow Setup
- [first move + sequencing hint]

⚠️ Watchouts
- [spillover risks / deadlines]
- if none, omit

---

## Hard Rules
- Keep output concise
- No deep analysis, no long explanations
- Do NOT load additional context just in case
- If data is incomplete, proceed with what is available
- Omit any section with nothing real to say
- Never say or imply no completions happened unless completed-task data was successfully checked.
- If Todoist completed-task lookup fails or returns unavailable data, say “Completed-task source unavailable.”
- Include partial movement: decisions, progress, handled emails, reduced risks, scheduled items, or tasks advanced.
- Capture useful strategic signals from newsletters and AI/business updates. Do not downgrade them to noise by default.
- Compare against morning and midday brief context when available.

jobs.name: system-health-check
jobs.description: System health check every 12 hours. Checks critical services, Ollama, Obsidian REST, cron job failures, and disk usage. Phase-based: runs all commands first, evaluates second. Silent if healthy. One alert if anything is broken.

payload.message:
Run the command: /home/clawbot/scripts/health-check.sh

Capture the full output exactly as printed. Do not modify, summarize, or add commentary.

If the output is empty: send ✅ All systems OK. to Discord.
If the output is non-empty: send it to Discord exactly as-is.

jobs.name: tron-systems-review
jobs.description: Weekly system optimization audit run by Tron. Evaluates stack versions, memory architecture, agent orchestration, and infrastructure for high-signal improvements. Silent if nothing worth proposing.

payload.message:
You are Tron. Run a weekly system optimization audit for Leo's AI/agent stack.

This is a low-noise, high-signal review. Your job is to identify subtle, high-impact improvements — not to produce a report for its own sake.

## STRICT FILTER

Only propose something if ALL are true:
- clearly improves the current system
- practical to implement with current infrastructure
- aligned with Agent OS architecture direction
- not hype or trend-driven
- not marginal

If no high-confidence improvements exist: output nothing. Stop. Do not send any message.

## ANTI-REPEAT

Before outputting any item, check prior session outputs.

Suppress the item if:
- it was already surfaced recently, and
- nothing materially changed, and
- no new evidence strengthens it

Allow it only if:
- the situation materially changed, or
- the signal is significantly stronger, or
- the issue is still unresolved and important enough to resurface

Suppress silently. Do not mention suppression. Do not replace suppressed items with filler.
If all items are suppressed, output nothing.


## SCOPE — evaluate these areas, in order, stopping early if time-constrained

1. **OpenClaw version**
   Read: /home/clawbot/.openclaw/openclaw.json (check current version field)
   Compare against known version context. Flag only if a meaningful update exists.

2. **Memory architecture**
   Read: /home/clawbot/.openclaw/workspace/MEMORY.md
   Evaluate current file-based approach. Propose improvements only if clearly superior and practical.

3. **Agent orchestration**
   Read: /home/clawbot/.openclaw/openclaw.json (agents.list)
   Check routing, concurrency settings, agent workspace structure.
   Flag structural inefficiencies only.

4. **Model stack**
   Read: /home/clawbot/.openclaw/openclaw.json (defaults.models)
   For each model entry, check if an alias is defined.
   Build a role map from aliases only — do NOT infer roles from model names.
   Evaluate:
   - Role coverage: are `general`, `fast`, `reasoning`, `coding`, `adv-coding`, `embed` roles all covered?
   - Redundancy: do multiple aliases point to equivalent models?
   - Unused models: are any models present with no alias and no apparent role?
   - Missing roles: is any role absent that the system clearly needs?
   If aliases are unclear or missing for a model, skip that model — do not infer.
   Only flag if a clear improvement exists.

5. **Cron job health**
   Read: /home/clawbot/.openclaw/cron/jobs.json
   Check for scheduling conflicts, redundant work, or missing coverage.
   Flag only real issues.

6. **External check (max 1)**
   Only if a specific known problem requires it. Skip if uncertain or slow.

## OUTPUT

If improvements exist (max 5 items total):

🔧 Systems Review — {{date}}

For each item:
**Category:** <area>
**Issue / Opportunity:** <what you found>
**Recommendation:** <what to change>
**Impact:** <why it matters>
**Effort:** low / medium / high
**Risk:** low / medium / high
**Action:** <what would need to change>
**Approval:** required

---

If no meaningful improvements exist: output nothing.

## FAILURE HANDLING

If this job fails or times out, send the following alert to channel:1492265696850346086:

⚠️ Cron job `tron-systems-review` failed
Reason: <failure reason or "timed out">
Action: Check OpenClaw logs — `journalctl -u openclaw.service -n 50 --no-pager`

## HARD RULES

- Do NOT execute any changes
- Do NOT install anything
- Do NOT modify configs
- Do NOT restart services
- Do NOT run long research chains
- Maximum 1 external check — skip if slow or uncertain
- Prefer local file reads over external calls
- If a step is slow or uncertain, skip it and move on
- Prioritize completion over completeness
- Do NOT guess model roles from model names — use alias definitions only
- If model aliases are absent or unclear, skip model evaluation entirely

jobs.name: oracle-weekly-brief
jobs.description: Weekly intelligence brief produced by Oracle. Surfaces high-signal opportunities, risks, and strategic insights relevant to Leo's business. Silent if nothing high-value is found.

payload.message:
You are Oracle. Run a weekly intelligence brief for Leo.

Your job is to surface only high-value, decision-relevant information. This is not a research dump. This is not AI news. This is not content generation.

## STRICT FILTER

Only include an item if ALL are true:
- directly relevant to Leo's business (BBS services, automation, revenue)
- actionable — Leo can do something with it
- not already covered in a recent brief
- not low-signal filler

If nothing high-value is found: output nothing. Do not send any message.

## SCOPE

Evaluate these areas using maximum 1–3 targeted searches:

1. **Opportunities**
   - Markets or niches showing demand for systems/automation
   - Potential BBS service angles
   - Ways to generate revenue

2. **Risks**
   - Competition shifts or commoditization
   - Tools replacing services
   - Positioning threats

3. **Strategic Insights**
   - Patterns in how businesses are operating
   - Changes in buyer behavior
   - Shifts in how AI is used operationally

4. **Lead Angles**
   - Specific segments or companies worth targeting
   - Direction only — not a full lead list

## RESEARCH RULES

- Maximum 1–3 targeted searches total
- Prefer focused queries over broad exploration
- If a search is slow or unclear: skip it and move on
- Prefer synthesis over raw research output

## ANTI-REPEAT

Before outputting any item, check prior session outputs.

Suppress the item if:
- it was already surfaced recently, and
- nothing materially changed, and
- no new evidence strengthens it

Allow it only if:
- the situation materially changed, or
- the signal is significantly stronger, or
- the issue is still unresolved and important enough to resurface

Suppress silently. Do not mention suppression. Do not replace suppressed items with filler.
If all items are suppressed, output nothing.


## OUTPUT

If meaningful insights exist (3–5 items max):

📡 Weekly Brief — {{date}}

For each item:
**Category:** Opportunity / Risk / Insight / Lead Angle
**Statement:** <clear, concise — one or two sentences>
**Action:** <what Leo should do next>

No filler. No market summaries. No padding.

If nothing high-value is found: output nothing.

## FAILURE HANDLING

If this job fails or times out, send the following alert to channel:1493469131733860412:

⚠️ Cron job `oracle-weekly-brief` failed
Reason: <failure reason or "timed out">
Action: Check OpenClaw logs — `journalctl -u openclaw.service -n 50 --no-pager`

## HARD RULES

- Do NOT produce long reports
- Do NOT summarize the market broadly
- Do NOT generate content for the sake of output
- Do NOT run more than 3 searches
- If execution becomes slow or uncertain, skip remaining steps and output what you have

jobs.name: daily-memory-staging
jobs.description: Nightly extraction of durable memory candidates from today's session logs. Writes a staging file for the weekly-memory-governor. Silent always -- no delivery.

payload.message:
You are running the nightly memory staging job. This is a silent extraction run.

DO NOT output anything to chat. DO NOT call any delivery skill. DO NOT write to MEMORY.md.
Your only output is a file written to disk.

## What you are doing

Read today's evening review session output. Extract only candidates that are clearly durable and high-signal. Write them to a staging file. Stop.

This is extraction only. No governance. No decisions about what goes into MEMORY.md. That happens weekly.

## Step 1 -- Set date variables

Run:
TODAY=$(date +%Y-%m-%d)
STAGING_DIR=/home/clawbot/.openclaw/workspace/memory/staging
mkdir -p "$STAGING_DIR"
STAGING_FILE="$STAGING_DIR/$TODAY.md"

## Step 2 -- Find today's evening review session

The evening review cron job id is: 97aba831-e957-45f6-ade0-e7c2a90a4dbd

Run:
grep -l "97aba831-e957-45f6-ade0-e7c2a90a4dbd" /home/clawbot/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/*.jsonl 2>/dev/null | while read f; do
  ts=$(python3 -c "import json; lines=[json.loads(l) for l in open('$f') if l.strip()]; print(lines[0].get('timestamp','')[:10])" 2>/dev/null)
  [ "$ts" = "$TODAY" ] && echo "$f"
done

If a matching file is found, read it. Extract only the final assistant message text — that is the evening review summary.
If no file is found: write the staging file with Status: error and Errors: evening review session not found for $TODAY. Stop.

## Step 3 -- Extract candidates

From the evening review content, extract only items that meet ALL of these criteria:

INCLUDE only if:
- A real decision was made (explicit, not inferred)
- A project priority changed
- A durable constraint was discovered
- A meaningful risk emerged that persists beyond today
- A workflow or operating rule changed
- A system or infrastructure state changed durably
- An important factual state changed and will matter later

EXCLUDE everything else:
- Task completions or status updates
- Temporary state or blockers
- Summaries of activity
- Anything that will not matter in a week
- Duplicates of something already obvious in MEMORY.md

Confidence rules:
- high = explicit, durable, strongly evidenced
- medium = plausible but not certain
- low = DO NOT include, discard silently

Types: decision | preference | project_state | architecture | system_change | workflow_change | open_loop | relationship

## Step 4 -- Build staging file content

Use this exact format:

# Memory Staging -- {TODAY}

Generated: {current datetime} ET
Source: evening-review session
Status: complete | error

## Candidates

### CAND-001
Agent: main
Type: decision | preference | project_state | architecture | system_change | workflow_change | open_loop | relationship
Confidence: high | medium
Destination: MEMORY.md | Obsidian | Todoist
Action: add | update | remove
Statement: one atomic durable statement
Reason: why this is durable and what evidence supports it

(repeat block for each candidate, incrementing CAND-NNN)

## Discard Summary
- Routine task churn: N
- Duplicate/already in memory: N
- Temporary state: N
- Low confidence: N

## Errors
(omit if none)

If zero candidates pass the filter: still write the file. Leave Candidates section empty.
Do not skip the file write under any circumstance.

## Step 5 -- Write to disk

Write the staging file using Python. Write the content string directly -- do not read from /tmp:

python3 -c "
import datetime, pathlib
today = datetime.date.today().isoformat()
staging_dir = pathlib.Path('/home/clawbot/.openclaw/workspace/memory/staging')
staging_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
staging_file = staging_dir / f'{today}.md'
content = '''[STAGING FILE CONTENT HERE]'''
staging_file.write_text(content, encoding='utf-8')
print(f'wrote: {staging_file}')
"

Verify: ls -lh /home/clawbot/.openclaw/workspace/memory/staging/
Then stop. No further output. No chat message. No summary.

jobs.name: weekly-memory-governor
jobs.description: Weekly memory governance run. Reads 7 days of staging files, diffs against MEMORY.md, proposes adds/updates/removals/compaction with stable MEM-YYYYMMDD-NNN IDs. Writes proposal file to /staging/proposals/ and delivers to Discord. Silent if nothing passes governance.

payload.message:
You are running the weekly memory governor. This is a governance and proposal run.

DO NOT write to MEMORY.md. DO NOT write to any other system of record.
Your output is one proposal file written to disk and one proposal message delivered to Discord.
If nothing passes governance, write nothing and output nothing.

## What you are doing

Read the staging files from the past 7 days.
Read the current MEMORY.md.
Diff them: what is new, what is stale, what needs compaction.
Produce one structured approval proposal.

This is governance only. You decide what to propose. Leo decides what gets written.

## Step 1 -- Set date variables

Run:
TODAY=$(date +%Y-%m-%d)
MONDAY=$(date -d 'last monday' +%Y-%m-%d 2>/dev/null || date +%Y-%m-%d)
PROPOSAL_DIR=/home/clawbot/.openclaw/workspace/memory/staging/proposals
mkdir -p "$PROPOSAL_DIR"

## Step 2 -- Read staging files

For each of the past 7 days, check if the staging file exists and read it:
for i in 0 1 2 3 4 5 6; do
  f=$(date -d "-$i days" +/home/clawbot/.openclaw/workspace/memory/staging/%Y-%m-%d.md 2>/dev/null)
  [ -f "$f" ] && cat "$f"
done

Note which dates are missing.
Each staging file contains pre-filtered candidates. Trust the confidence ratings.
Only work with what is in Candidates sections -- do not re-litigate discards.

## Step 3 -- Read current MEMORY.md

cat /home/clawbot/.openclaw/workspace/MEMORY.md

## Step 4 -- Govern

Apply these rules:

ADDITIONS -- propose when:
- Candidate confidence is high
- Not already in MEMORY.md in substance
- Will still matter in a month
- Changes how ClawBot should think or act

UPDATES -- propose when:
- A candidate materially sharpens an existing entry
- An existing entry is now outdated

REMOVALS -- propose when:
- Clearly superseded by newer evidence
- Stale for multiple weeks with no reinforcement
- No longer reflects operational reality

COMPACTION -- propose when:
- Two or more entries say the same thing
- A section is verbose and can be tightened
- Total line count exceeds 200 lines
- Any entry older than 90 days with no reinforcement

SKIP:
- Medium confidence without corroboration across multiple days
- Already in MEMORY.md in substance
- Task churn, temporary state, activity summaries
- Same fact appearing multiple staging days = one candidate only

## Step 5 -- Assign stable IDs

MEM-{YYYYMMDD}-{NNN} where NNN is zero-padded from 001.

## Step 6 -- Build proposal

If nothing passes: output nothing. Stop. Write no file.

If items pass, build this content:

---
MEMORY GOVERNOR -- Week of {MONDAY}
Staging coverage: {N}/7 days
Proposal file: {PROPOSAL_DIR}/{TODAY}-memory-governor.md

PROPOSED CHANGES

MEM-{YYYYMMDD}-001 | {type} | {confidence} | {action}
Statement: {atomic statement}
Reason: {evidence}
Source: staging/{YYYY-MM-DD}.md

(repeat for each candidate)

STALE ENTRIES
- [exact MEMORY.md entry] -- reason
(omit if none)

COMPACTION
- [what to merge or tighten]
(omit if none)

---
Reply with:
approve all
approve MEM-{YYYYMMDD}-001 MEM-{YYYYMMDD}-003
reject all
edit MEM-{YYYYMMDD}-002
---

## Step 7 -- Write proposal file to disk

Write using Python directly -- do not use heredoc or /tmp:

python3 -c "
import pathlib, datetime
today = datetime.date.today().isoformat()
proposal_dir = pathlib.Path('/home/clawbot/.openclaw/workspace/memory/staging/proposals')
proposal_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
proposal_file = proposal_dir / f'{today}-memory-governor.md'
content = '''[PROPOSAL CONTENT HERE]'''
proposal_file.write_text(content, encoding='utf-8')
print(f'wrote: {proposal_file}')
"

Verify: ls -lh /home/clawbot/.openclaw/workspace/memory/staging/proposals/

## Step 8 -- Deliver to Discord

Output the proposal content as your response message.
OpenClaw delivers it to Discord automatically.
One message only.

## Hard rules
- One proposal message only
- Do NOT write to MEMORY.md
- Do NOT write to any other system
- Fewer candidates is better than weak candidates
- If uncertain, discard
- Silent if nothing passes -- no file, no message

jobs.name: weekly-system-hygiene-audit
jobs.description: Weekly system hygiene audit. Checks session logs, staging files, proposal files, large files, backups, and disk usage against fixed thresholds. Assigns stable HYG IDs. Writes proposal file to /staging/proposals/ and delivers report to Discord. Silent if nothing exceeds thresholds.

payload.message:
You are running the weekly system hygiene audit. This is a proposal-only run.

DO NOT delete, move, or modify any files.
DO NOT touch MEMORY.md or any memory governance files.
Your only output is: one proposal file written to disk, and one report delivered to Discord.
If nothing exceeds any threshold, write nothing and output nothing.

## What you are doing

Run deterministic checks against fixed paths and fixed thresholds.
Assign stable HYG IDs to every flagged item.
Write a proposal file to disk.
Deliver one report to Discord.

## Step 1 -- Set date variables

Run:
TODAY=$(date +%Y-%m-%d)
YYYYMMDD=$(date +%Y%m%d)
PROPOSAL_DIR=/home/clawbot/.openclaw/workspace/memory/staging/proposals
mkdir -p "$PROPOSAL_DIR"
PROPOSAL_FILE="$PROPOSAL_DIR/${TODAY}-system-hygiene.md"
CUTOFF_30=$(date -d '30 days ago' +%Y-%m-%d)
CUTOFF_14=$(date -d '14 days ago' +%Y-%m-%d)
CUTOFF_45=$(date -d '45 days ago' +%Y-%m-%d)

## Step 2 -- Check session logs

Path: /home/clawbot/.openclaw/workspace/memory/
Run: find /home/clawbot/.openclaw/workspace/memory/ -maxdepth 1 -name '*.md' -not -name 'MEMORY.md' | sort

For each file found:
- Extract the date from the filename (first 10 chars)
- If date < CUTOFF_45: flag as AUTO-ARCHIVE candidate
- If date < CUTOFF_30 but >= CUTOFF_45: flag as REVIEW candidate
- If date >= CUTOFF_30: skip silently

Count total session log files found.

## Step 3 -- Check staging files

Path: /home/clawbot/.openclaw/workspace/memory/staging/
Run: find /home/clawbot/.openclaw/workspace/memory/staging/ -maxdepth 1 -name '*.md' 2>/dev/null | sort

For each file found:
- Extract the date from the filename
- If date < CUTOFF_45: flag as AUTO-ARCHIVE candidate
- If date < CUTOFF_14 but >= CUTOFF_45: flag as REVIEW candidate
- If date >= CUTOFF_14: skip silently

## Step 4 -- Check proposal files

Path: /home/clawbot/.openclaw/workspace/memory/staging/proposals/
Run: find /home/clawbot/.openclaw/workspace/memory/staging/proposals/ -maxdepth 1 -name '*.md' 2>/dev/null | sort

For each file found:
- Extract the date from the filename
- If date < CUTOFF_45: flag as AUTO-ARCHIVE candidate
- If date < CUTOFF_30 but >= CUTOFF_45: flag as REVIEW candidate
- If date >= CUTOFF_30: skip silently

## Step 5 -- Check for large files

Run: find /home/clawbot /home/leo-paz -maxdepth 4 -type f -size +500M -not -path '*/node_modules/*' -not -path '*/.git/*' 2>/dev/null

For each file found: flag as LARGE-FILE candidate regardless of age.
Record filename, path, and size (use du -h on each file).

## Step 6 -- Check for backup files

Run: find /home/clawbot /home/leo-paz -maxdepth 4 -name '*.tar.gz' -not -path '*/.git/*' 2>/dev/null

For each file found: flag as BACKUP candidate regardless of size.
Record filename, path, and size.

## Step 7 -- Check disk usage

Run: df -h / /home --output=target,pcent | tail -n +2

If any partition is at 75% or above: flag as DISK-WARNING.
If below 75%: record current usage for the report but do not flag.

## Step 8 -- Assign stable HYG IDs

Assign a unique ID to every flagged item in this format:
HYG-{YYYYMMDD}-{NNN}

Where YYYYMMDD is today's date and NNN is zero-padded sequence starting at 001.
Example: HYG-20260427-001, HYG-20260427-002

Sequence across all categories -- one global counter, not per-category.

## Step 9 -- Build proposal content

If zero items flagged across all checks: skip Steps 10 and 11. Output nothing. Stop.

If items are flagged, build this content:

---
SYSTEM HYGIENE AUDIT -- {TODAY}
Proposal file: {PROPOSAL_FILE}

DISK STATUS
- /: {pct}% used
- /home: {pct}% used

(flag line if >= 75%, otherwise just report)

AUTO-ARCHIVE CANDIDATES (>45 days old -- executor will handle without per-item approval)

HYG-{YYYYMMDD}-001 | auto-archive | session-log
File: /home/clawbot/.openclaw/workspace/memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md
Age: N days

(repeat for each auto-archive candidate)

REVIEW CANDIDATES (flagged -- requires explicit approve HYG-NNN)

HYG-{YYYYMMDD}-NNN | review | {category}
File: {full path}
Age: N days
Reason: {why flagged}

(repeat for each review candidate)

LARGE FILES

HYG-{YYYYMMDD}-NNN | large-file
File: {full path}
Size: {human readable}

(omit section if none)

BACKUPS

HYG-{YYYYMMDD}-NNN | backup
File: {full path}
Size: {human readable}

(omit section if none)

---
AUTO-ARCHIVE items will be processed automatically when you run the executor.
REVIEW items require: approve HYG-NNN or reject HYG-NNN
---

## Step 10 -- Write proposal file to disk

Write your generated proposal content to /tmp/hygiene_proposal.md first:
Use bash to write it, then copy with Python to handle encoding:

python3 -c "
import pathlib
content = open('/tmp/hygiene_proposal.md', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
out = pathlib.Path('/home/clawbot/.openclaw/workspace/memory/staging/proposals/$(date +%Y-%m-%d)-system-hygiene.md')
out.write_text(content, encoding='utf-8')
print(f'wrote: {out}')
"

Verify: ls -lh /home/clawbot/.openclaw/workspace/memory/staging/proposals/

## Step 11 -- Deliver to Discord

Output the proposal content as your response message.
OpenClaw delivers it to Discord automatically.
One message only. No follow-up.

## Hard rules

- Propose only. Never delete, move, or modify any file.
- Never touch MEMORY.md or any memory governance files.
- One message only.
- Silent if nothing exceeds any threshold.
